steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea. The passage introduces Darcie Little Badger's blending of Apache stories with popular genres, then pivots with “But Little Badger is not the only Indigenous writer to blend traditional stories with popular literature” — making that broader pattern the central claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Several famous Indigenous writers increased their book sales when they began incorporating traditional stories.': book sales are never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Recent Indigenous fiction writers are mixing traditional stories with popular literary genres.': directly restates the passage's main point, supported by both Little Badger and Nathan Adler. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The success of Darcie Little Badger has influenced non-Indigenous authors who write in popular literary genres.': influence on non-Indigenous authors is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Non-Indigenous authors should consider the expectations of Indigenous readers.': no advice to non-Indigenous authors appears anywhere in the passage. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
